__tinydesc: Parse and generate YAML data format
__description: The yaml module provides YAML data format reading/writing capabilities

null.__description: Read-only property representing a YAML null value. When encoded, produces a YAML null literal, and when decoding a YAML null, results in this value.

decode.__description:
Parses the given YAML string and returns the resulting Lua value. This function supports multiple documents, placeholder substitution, anchors, and type conversion.

Placeholders in the form {{ var }} can be replaced with values from the provided table, from local variables, upvalues, or globals. Unresolved placeholders become yaml.null.

Conversion to Lua values follows this rules:
YAML Lua value type
"string" "string"
number number
boolean boolean
YAML mapping {} table
YAML sequence [] table (integer keys)
null yaml.null

__params.__description: An optional table of placeholder names to values. If omited, local variables, upvalues and globals will be used for resolution.

decode.__returns:
Returns the decoded Lua value (table, string, number, boolean, or yaml.null).

A table of documents is returned if multiple documents were found.

On error, returns nil and an error message string
decode.__example:
local yaml = require "yaml"

local data = yaml.decode("name: Alice")
print(data.name)  -- Alice

local template = "foo: {{ var }}"
local vars = { var = "hello" }
local filled = yaml.decode(template, vars)
print(filled.foo) -- hello

encode.__tinydesc: Convert a Lua table to YAML string representation
encode.__description:
Encodes the given Lua table into a YAML string.

This function supports sequences (table with integer keys), mappings (tables with string keys), multiple documents (when given an array of tables), and preserves types following this rules :
Lua value type YAML
"string" "string"
number number
boolean boolean
table (non array) YAML mapping {}
table (integer keys) YAML sequence []
yaml.null null
object Not supported (raises an error)
thread Not supported (raises an error)
userdata Not supported (raises an error)

__params.__description: The Lua table to encode to YAML string.

encode.__returns: Returns the YAML representation as a string.
encode.__example:
local yaml = require "yaml"

local t = {name = "Alice", age = 30}
local ystr = yaml.encode(t)
print(ystr)

load.__tinydesc: Load and decode YAML data from a file
load.__description: Loads and decodes YAML content from a file. See the yaml.decode() for more information on YAML decoding.

__params.__description: A File object or a filename string, representing the YAML file.
An optional table of placeholder names to values. If omited, local variables, upvalues and globals will be used for resolution.

load.__returns: Returns the parsed Lua value or table of documents. On error, returns nil and an error message string.
load.__example:
local yaml = require "yaml"

local data, err = yaml.load("config.yaml")
if data then
  print(data.setting)
else
  print("Error:", err)
end

save.__tinydesc: Encode and save a lua table in YAML format to a file
save.__description: Encodes a Lua table into YAML and writes it to a file. Returns true and nil on success, or false and an error message on failure.

__params.__description: A File object or a filename string, representing the YAML file.

save.__returns: On success, returns true. On failure, returns false and an error message string
save.__example:
local yaml = require "yaml"

local t = {name = "Bob", age = 42}
local ok, err = yaml.save("person.yaml", t)
if not ok then print("Save failed:", err) end
